Title: A Latex Metabolite Benefits Plant Fitness under Root Herbivore Attack

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: terpene synthase; Asteraceae germacrene A synthase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Deters root herbivore feeding and contributes to plant fitness under attack through TA-G accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in latex and main root, but lower than ToGAS1; low in outer root cortex
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in latex and main root of A34 plants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: root herbivory context; transcriptome and biochemical characterization
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome identification; heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assay with FDP; RT-qPCR; RNAi specificity analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ToGAS2 was identified as a second putative germacrene A synthase from T. officinale. 2. Recombinant ToGAS2 produced (+)-germacrene A from FDP in E. coli assays. 3. Its transcript abundance was lower than ToGAS1 in latex and main roots. 4. ToGAS2 expression was not reduced in ToGAS1 RNAi lines, and TA-G depletion was attributed mainly to ToGAS1 suppression.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional germacrene A synthase candidate, but not established as the main in planta determinant of TA-G accumulation in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although biochemically active, ToGAS2 was not the primary target controlling TA-G levels under the tested conditions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome sequencing of latex and main root; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA301484

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: A34; RNAi-1; RNAi-12b; RNAi-16; RNAi-9; RNAi-15; WT
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: terpene synthase; Asteraceae germacrene A synthase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: TA-G branch from farnesyl diphosphate-derived sesquiterpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts farnesyl diphosphate (FDP/FPP) to (+)-germacrene A, the first committed intermediate toward TA-G biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool; catalyzes first committed step toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Root defense against herbivory; maintenance of vegetative and reproductive performance under attack
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in latex and entire main root, with stronger expression than ToGAS2; low in outer root cortex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in latex and main root of A34 plants
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as first committed and key control step for TA-G biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: root herbivory by Melolontha melolontha; common garden infestation; artificial diet assay context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing in planta; heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assay with FDP; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling of TA-G; herbivore choice and no-choice assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ToGAS1 was identified from root/latex transcriptome and encodes a functional germacrene A synthase. 2. Recombinant ToGAS1 converted FDP to (+)-germacrene A in E. coli assays. 3. ToGAS1 is more highly expressed than ToGAS2 in latex and main root. 4. RNAi silencing of ToGAS1 reduced TA-G by >90% without suppressing ToGAS2. 5. ToGAS1-silenced lines were preferred by M. melolontha and lost more root biomass, linking ToGAS1-dependent TA-G biosynthesis to defense and plant fitness.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TA-G accumulation; increases deterrence of M. melolontha; reduces herbivore-caused root biomass loss; supports maintenance of plant fitness under attack
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance root chemical defense by increasing TA-G, but likely should be engineered with caution because silencing affected PIE levels and growth, suggesting metabolic tradeoffs around FPP flux
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing caused ~50% lower root mass in some lines, altered total root protein levels, and reduced PIE accumulation, suggesting off-target flux effects on related metabolism and growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TA-G is predominantly stored in laticifers and is constitutively produced rather than induced by herbivory. Authors note ToGAS1 silencing may alter FDP pool sizes and affect sesquiterpene, triterpene, phytosterol or growth-related metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome sequencing of latex and main root; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA301484
